window.label: Entire history
window.phrase: the entire available history up to 2026-07-22

evidence.claim: On August 28, 2024, the UAE dispatched an emergency relief aircraft carrying 50 tons of humanitarian aid for flood victims in Bangladesh.
evidence.snippet: Following devastating monsoon floods, the UAE sent an emergency flight carrying vital medical supplies, food, and shelter materials to Dhaka.

evidence.claim: Bangladesh and the UAE convened a round of CEPA bilateral trade negotiations in Abu Dhabi on September 14, 2024.
evidence.snippet: Trade officials from Bangladesh and the UAE met to advance the framework for a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement.

evidence.claim: AD Ports Group signed a memorandum of understanding with the Chittagong Port Authority on November 10, 2024, to modernize Laldia Terminal.
evidence.snippet: AD Ports Group agreed to evaluate major operations and logistics modernization opportunities at the multi-purpose Laldia Terminal.

evidence.claim: A UAE trade delegation in Dhaka announced plans for a USD 300 million investment in Bangladesh's textile logistics hubs on December 5, 2024.
evidence.snippet: A delegation of Abu Dhabi business leaders discussed a USD 300 million investment to bolster Bangladesh's apparel export logistics.

evidence.claim: flydubai expanded its aviation network in Bangladesh by adding three weekly flights to Chittagong, starting January 22, 2025.
evidence.snippet: flydubai expanded its regional capacity to Chittagong, adding three new weekly flights to serve growing expatriate worker traffic.

evidence.claim: UAE-based LuLu Group launched its first direct agricultural sourcing and export hub in Dhaka on February 18, 2025.
evidence.snippet: LuLu Group opened an export-oriented logistics and food processing facility to source agricultural products directly from Bangladesh.

evidence.claim: The UAE Ministry of Human Resources signed an agreement with Bangladesh on March 5, 2025, to regulate digital recruitment processes.
evidence.snippet: The UAE and Bangladesh launched a joint digital initiative to protect workers' rights and streamline formal recruitment processes.

evidence.claim: Masdar and the Bangladesh Power Development Board finalized a joint venture for a 100MW solar park in Chittagong on April 12, 2025.
evidence.snippet: Masdar signed a formal agreement to construct a utility-scale 100-megawatt solar photovoltaic power plant in Chittagong.

evidence.claim: The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) allocated a USD 35 million development grant to Bangladesh's healthcare sector on May 10, 2025.
evidence.snippet: ADFD committed a USD 35 million development grant to finance the upgrade of rural clinics and community healthcare units.

evidence.claim: DP World initiated discussions with Bangladesh's Ministry of Shipping on June 22, 2025, regarding a USD 150 million inland dry port.
evidence.snippet: DP World proposed a USD 150 million development plan to construct modern inland container depots near Dhaka to reduce port congestion.

edges.claim: On August 28, 2024, the UAE dispatched an emergency relief aircraft carrying 50 tons of humanitarian aid for flood victims in Bangladesh.
Bangladesh and the UAE convened a round of CEPA bilateral trade negotiations in Abu Dhabi on September 14, 2024.
AD Ports Group signed a memorandum of understanding with the Chittagong Port Authority on November 10, 2024, to modernize Laldia Terminal.
UAE-based LuLu Group launched its first direct agricultural sourcing and export hub in Dhaka on February 18, 2025.
flydubai expanded its aviation network in Bangladesh by adding three weekly flights to Chittagong, starting January 22, 2025.
The UAE Ministry of Human Resources signed an agreement with Bangladesh on March 5, 2025, to regulate digital recruitment processes.
Masdar and the Bangladesh Power Development Board finalized a joint venture for a 100MW solar park in Chittagong on April 12, 2025.
The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) allocated a USD 35 million development grant to Bangladesh's healthcare sector on May 10, 2025.
DP World initiated discussions with Bangladesh's Ministry of Shipping on June 22, 2025, regarding a USD 150 million inland dry port.
Co-mentioned in the same evidence record (E3) — plausible unstated relationship.
Co-mentioned in the same evidence record (E5) — plausible unstated relationship.
